answer:
**Manu's ordering of the four varṇas** (from *Manusmṛti* 1) originates in the primordial Puruṣa's body parts, establishing a fixed hierarchy from highest to lowest: Brāhmaṇa (mouth), Kṣatriya (arms), Vaiśya (thighs), and Śūdra (feet).[^1] Their respective duties reinforce this: Brāhmaṇas teach and study the Veda, perform sacrifices for themselves and others, and give/receive alms; Kṣatriyas protect subjects while giving, sacrificing, studying, and renouncing sense objects; Vaiśyas tend cattle, give, sacrifice, study, trade, lend, and farm; Śūdras serve the others without resentment.[^2]

**Viṣṇu's ordering** (from *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 2, an early chapter) simply lists them sequentially from highest to lowest: Brāhmaṇa, Kṣatriya, Vaiśya, and Śūdra.[^3] Duties are more concise: Brāhmaṇas teach; Kṣatriyas train with arms; Vaiśyas tend cattle; Śūdras serve the twice-born.[^4] (The twice-born—Brāhmaṇa, Kṣatriya, Vaiśya—also share sacrificing for others and studying as common duties.[^5])

Viṣṇu's treatment admits **more mobility between varṇas**, explicitly permitting adoption of the *next lower varṇa's livelihood* during times of distress (e.g., a Brāhmaṇa could take up Kṣatriya work, etc.).[^6] No such exception appears in Manu's Chapter 1 catalogue.

**Rigidity shows most explicitly in Manu**, via the irreversible body-part origins (implying innate superiority, with the mouth "purest") and the Śūdra's singular, subservient duty—serving by birth alone, with no reciprocal obligations or upward paths mentioned here.[^7][^8]

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-13a2db](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-13a2db).
[^2]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-71a5c2](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-71a5c2); [/translation/#p-a09d4d](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-a09d4d); [/translation/#p-357321](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-357321); [/translation/#p-26a572](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-26a572).
[^3]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 2/translation/#p-883be2](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-883be2).
[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 2/translation/#p-354ee5](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-354ee5); [/translation/#p-f4e97b](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-f4e97b); [/translation/#p-6910e1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-6910e1); [/translation/#p-f34593](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-f34593).
[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 2/translation/#p-321ed8](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-321ed8).
[^6]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 2/translation/#p-600695](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/02/translation/#p-600695).
[^7]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-4809ee](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-4809ee).
[^8]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 1/translation/#p-26a572](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-26a572).
